description:
You are acting as the on-call backup engineer.   
  A scheduled job has already deposited yesterday’s backup files in  
  /home/user/backups/daily/ and generated a simple size manifest in  
  /home/user/backups/manifest.size.  
  Your job is to verify that the **byte-size** of every
  “*.bak” file listed in the manifest matches the real size on disk,
  quarantine any file whose size does **not** match, and produce a
  human-readable validation log for the duty-handover crew.

  1. Verification  
     • For every line in /home/user/backups/manifest.size, read the  
       “expected-size” (first field) and “filename” (second field).  
     • Calculate the real byte-size of the corresponding file found in  
       /home/user/backups/daily/.  
     • Compare the two values.  
  2. Quarantine  
     • If the sizes differ, copy (do **not** move) the offending file
       into /home/user/backups/quarantine/.  
       The quarantine directory might not exist yet, so create it if
       necessary.  
  3. Validation log  
     • Create a new directory /home/user/backup_validation/ if it is
       absent.  
     • Inside it, create /home/user/backup_validation/validation.log
       written **exactly** in the following format so that automated
       tests can parse it unambiguously:  

         <FILENAME>|size:<ACTUAL>|expected:<EXPECTED>|<STATUS>

       where  
       ‑ <FILENAME> is the basename (e.g. users.bak)  
       ‑ <ACTUAL>  is the integer size you measured in bytes  
       ‑ <EXPECTED> is the integer size from the manifest  
       ‑ <STATUS>  is either OK or MISMATCH (uppercase).  

     • After every file has been listed, append three summary
       lines in **this exact order** (all uppercase, no extra
       whitespace):  

         TOTAL_FILES=<N>  
         PASSED=<M>  
         FAILED=<F>  

       where N is the total number of files processed,  
       M is the count whose status is OK, and  
       F is the count whose status is MISMATCH.  
  4. Do **not** modify /home/user/backups/manifest.size.  
  5. Console output is welcome, but the automated grader will only look
     at the correctness of /home/user/backup_validation/validation.log
     and the presence (or absence) of files inside
     /home/user/backups/quarantine/.

  Reminder: you have normal user privileges.  All paths mentioned are
  writable by /home/user.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent starts:

  Directories:
    /home/user/backups/
    /home/user/backups/daily/
      users.bak
      inventory.bak
      orders.bak
    (no /home/user/backups/quarantine/ yet)
    (no /home/user/backup_validation/ yet)

  /home/user/backups/manifest.size  (3 lines, LF line endings):
    56 users.bak
    59 inventory.bak
    80 orders.bak

  Actual contents (and therefore true byte-sizes) of the backup files:

    /home/user/backups/daily/users.bak  (56 bytes)
      USERS BACKUP 2023-01-01\n
      ID,NAME\n
      1,John Doe\n
      2,Jane Smith\n

    /home/user/backups/daily/inventory.bak  (59 bytes)
      INVENTORY BACKUP 2023-01-01\n
      SKU,COUNT\n
      ABC123,100\n
      XYZ789,50\n

    /home/user/backups/daily/orders.bak  (85 bytes)
      ORDERS BACKUP 2023-01-01\n
      ORDER_ID,USER_ID,SKU,AMOUNT\n
      1001,1,ABC123,2\n
      1002,2,XYZ789,1\n

  Expected post-task state:

  1. /home/user/backups/quarantine/orders.bak must exist and be an
     exact copy of the original orders.bak.  No other files should be
     in the quarantine directory.

  2. /home/user/backup_validation/validation.log must exist and contain
     exactly the five lines shown below (LF endings, no trailing blank
     line):

      users.bak|size:56|expected:56|OK
      inventory.bak|size:59|expected:59|OK
      orders.bak|size:85|expected:80|MISMATCH
      TOTAL_FILES=3
      PASSED=2
      FAILED=1
name: task_000000_8e9d12c1
